summary: Gruber catches Reuters reporter Gina Keating citing unnamed 'media industry experts' who called Walmart's movie download service a 'game changer,' then traces the quote back to a single source: Rob Enderle. He points out that Enderle has a track record of being spectacularly wrong about technology predictions, yet major media reporters continue to treat him as a credible analyst. Gruber argues that Enderle's persistent wrongness should disqualify him as an expert source, and that reporters who rely on him are simply cutting corners. The post uses the Walmart download service's quick demise as further evidence of Enderle's unreliability.
key_points: Keating's Reuters article attributed the 'game changer' label to plural 'media industry experts' when it actually came from a single source: Rob Enderle
Enderle predicted Walmart's download service would trigger 'a long decline' for DVD sales, but the service was canceled in under a year
Gruber argues Enderle's consistent wrongness should disqualify him from being cited as an expert, yet major reporters keep quoting him
The real problem isn't Enderle himself but the lazy journalism that treats him as a credible source despite his track record
money_quotes: If being completely wrong all the time doesn't disqualify Enderle as an expert, what would?
If Enderle were just another crackpot with an obscure weblog or column, I'd just sweep him into the 'pay no mind' file.

The only way it would be worthwhile for tech reporters to continue to press Enderle for quotes would be if they were willing to describe him as 'almost always utterly wrong', thus letting readers know that the opposite of what he claims is probably the case.
Enderle's presence is a warning sign. I see a quote from him I get the message. The reporter is out of ideas and has decided to cut corners.
The reason it's worth calling him out, repeatedly, is that he continues to hold sway with major media tech reporters.
key_insight: When reporters cite perpetually wrong analysts as 'experts,' the real failure isn't the analyst's incompetence but the journalist's laziness in not vetting their sources' track records.
